title: CFPB HMDA Application Count by Loan Type — Home Purchase 2018-2025

key_observations.conventional_conforming: Highly rate-sensitive — peaked ~350K/quarter (2021), now ~240K. Tracks interest rates closely.
key_observations.fha: Rate-insensitive — stable 80-100K/quarter throughout 2018-2025 regardless of rate environment.

ofi_finding.explanation: FHA applications are stable at ~80-100K/quarter regardless of rate environment. At 22.4% national denial rate, approximately 18-22K FHA borrowers are denied every quarter — consistently, regardless of market conditions.
ofi_finding.implication: This is not cyclical denial. This is structural overlay. The denial floor does not move because lender policy does not move.
ofi_finding.contrast: Conventional applications collapsed 60%+ when rates rose. FHA applications held steady — because FHA borrowers have no alternative. They cannot move up to conventional. They can only be denied.
ofi_finding.ofi_confirmation: Stable application volume + stable denial rate = stable overlay. OFI measures this structural friction, not cyclical market noise.

structural_denial_floor_estimate.note: ~80,000 FHA home purchase applicants denied annually due to overlay — not agency rules
